data.body:
BRAINSTORM decision (CONFIRMED by user) — front-matter comments tradeoff.

Machine-managed prompt front-matter will be CANONICAL-serialized (fixed key order, deterministic dump) and will NOT preserve inline YAML comments. APPROVED rationale (user): git history + ticket history supply any meta context needed outside of front-matter and prompt text, so comments inside front-matter are unnecessary. Consequence: we can BUILD a minimal canonical front-matter writer over the already-core PyYAML dep (no ruamel/python-frontmatter) and only ever rewrite the front-matter block, leaving the .md prompt body byte-for-byte. (Open follow-on flagged: must still preserve UNKNOWN/foreign front-matter keys on write-back for forward-compat, and handle line-endings/trailing-newline — round-trip corruptors peers guard against.)

NOTE: this is a genuine user-confirmed decision (unlike the retracted subagent verdicts). Still UNDER DISCUSSION per user pressure-test: (a) whether v1.1 'deep subtype check' is the right approach at all given no standard/lib exists; (b) ajv vs alternatives for in-editor validation; (c) experiments to derisk; (d) overall approach vs OSS editor-round-trip + prompt-library peers.
